messages.content:
Which of the following about Johnson’s rule is false?

Johnson’s rule does not give the value of make-span.

Johnson’s rule gives one optimal solution.

Johnson’s rule is applied for flow shop situation with deterministic processing time.

Johnson’s rule gives an approximate solution.
Johnson’s rule gives an approximate solution. (This statement is false as Johnson's rule gives an optimal solution)

Which of the following is used in the materials requirements planning (MRP) calculation?

Projected on hand.

Net requirements.

Planned-order-receipt.

All other choices.
All other choices. (Projected on hand, net requirements, and planned-order-receipt are all used in the materials requirements planning (MRP) calculation)

In Just in Time, the objective of Poka-Yoke is to

improve communication.

reduce changeover time.

reduce number of suppliers.

prevent or avoid mistakes.
prevent or avoid mistakes.

Which of the following rules gives always the minimum average job lateness?

Earliest due date.

Shortest processing time.

First come, first served.

Longest processing time.
Shortest processing time.

Which one of these is a system for reducing changeover time?

SMED.

TPM.

JIT.

Poka-yoke
SMED.

Which of the following is a factor that can affect the location selection?

Proximity to markets

Proximity to raw materials and suppliers

Labor costs

All other choices

All other choices. (In addition to proximity to markets, proximity to raw materials and suppliers, and labor costs, other factors that can affect location selection include infrastructure, governmental regulations and incentives, availability of transportation, local community support, and quality of life for employees.)

Who of the following is mainly responsible for determining HOW the product is to be produced?

Product designer

Business owner

Process designer

Schedule designer
Process designer.

Decisions regarding the products to be produced are generally made by upper-level management based on input from…

Marketing

Manufacturing

Finance

All other choices
Marketing.

Which of the following is NOT part of the facilities design component of the facilities planning?

Handling system design

Facility systems design

Facilities location

Layout design
Facilities location (Facilities location is a separate component of facilities planning, not a part of facilities design)

Which of the following statement is desirable in designing a process?

Fewer defect at process that are at the beginning of the manufacturing steps

Fewer defect at process that are in the middle of the manufacturing steps

Fewer defect at process that are near the end of the manufacturing steps

None of the above

Fewer defect at process that are at the beginning of the manufacturing steps. (It is desirable to have fewer defects at process that are at the beginning of the manufacturing steps as this can reduce rework and scrap costs down the line.)

Why would a bill of materials be helpful in designing a facility?

Lists all the parts required to produce a product

Help in determining the storage requirements at each station

Help in determining the size of the receiving area

All other choices
Lists all the parts required to produce a product.

Monte Carlo simulation can be used when a the model is …. and process input is ….

Dynamic and Stochastic

Static and Stochastic

Dynamic and Deterministic

Static and Deterministic
Dynamic and Stochastic.

Assume you have collected the interarrival time for XYZ System, and you have tested them in Stat::Fit or any other Distribution Fitting Software to get the best probability distribution that fits your data. Based on these 4 histograms, which probability distribution will best fit your data?




Exponential and Normal

Exponential and Pareto

Uniform and Normal

Uniform and Pareto
Exponential and Pareto.

a document that is created by simulationist to describe exactly what will be delivered, when, how, and by whom” is the definition of …

Stakeholder

Functional specification

Word document

Object
Functional specification.

If customers arrival randomly in a queueing system, the arrival process in this system is described as …

Discrete process

Continuous process

Stochastic process

Deterministic process
Stochastic process.

Assume you have collected the interarrival time for XYZ System, and you have tested them in Stat::Fit or any other Distribution Fitting Software to get the best probability distribution that fits your data. Based the Chi-square goodness of fit test with α= 0.05, which probability distribution will best fit your data?





Uniform

Pareto

Normal

Exponential
Exponential.

Based on this queueing system, How many entities are currently in the queueing system?





6 Entities

4 Entities

7 Entities

3 Entities
3 Entities. (There are 3 entities in the queueing system: one in service, one in the pre-service area, and one in the queue)
The            is used to represent in influence diagram
Arrow.

To make an analytical model of customers needing service at cashier counter at panda, we will use the model based on.

Game Theory

Queuing Theory

Simulation model

Decision Tree
Queuing Theory.

In the given Cumulative Risk Profile, which variable stochastically dominates all others.




A

B

C

D
C.

In the given Cumulative Risk Profile, which variable stochastically dominates all others.
https://maharat.tu.edu.sa/pluginfile.php/3716639/question/questiontext/76094/91/81640/image.png
Variable D stochastically dominates all others.

The saddle point should be ---- in a fair game.

0

≠ 0

+1

∞

Decision making under certainty has.

only one state of nature

more than one state of nature

unknown state of nature

one or more than one state of nature
Only one state of nature.

The level of risk is high in decision making system which has.

Low mean

High mean

Low std. deviation

High std.Deviation
High standard deviation.

In a comparison of the cleaning action of four detergents, 20 pieces of white cloth were first soiled with India ink. The cloths were then washed under controlled conditions with 5 pieces washed by each of the detergents. Which of the following statistical test should be used?

One-way ANOVA.

Two-way ANOVA.

Paired t-test.

two-samples t-test
One-way ANOVA.

In a comparison of the cleaning action of four detergents, 20 pieces of white cloth were first soiled with India ink. The cloths were then washed under controlled conditions with 5 pieces washed by each of the detergents. Which of the following statistical test should be used?

One-way ANOVA.

Two-way ANOVA.

Paired t-test.

two-samples t-test
One-way ANOVA.

In the full factorial design 23 with 2 replications, the degree of freedom of the error is equal to (after considering all main effects and interactions) ?

15

12

9

3

3. 

The degree of freedom of the error is equal to (total number of runs - number of factors - 1). 

In this case, the total number of runs is 8 (2 replications of a 2-level design with 3 factors). 

The number of factors is 3. 

Therefore, the degree of freedom of the error is 8 - 3 - 1 = 4 - 1 = 3.

A result is called "statistically significant" whenever

The null hypothesis is true.

The alternative hypothesis is true.

The p-value is less or equal to the significance level.

The p-value is larger than the significance level
The p-value is less or equal to the significance level.

What is the acronym of ANOVA?

Analysis of Novel Variables.

Analogical Reasoning of Variance.

Analysis of Variance.

Analysis of Variables
Analysis of Variance.

In Design of Experiments, why do we perform experiments in a random order?

To increase degree of freedom of error.

To separate the main effects from interaction.

To increase the precision of an experiment.

To minimize the effect of uncontrolled (unknown) variables
To minimize the effect of uncontrolled (unknown) variables.
